title: Olearia ramulosa
intro: Olearia ramulosa, also known as bush daisy or woolly daisy, is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its compact growth habit and clusters of white or pink daisy-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Olearia ramulosa include phenolic compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Olearia ramulosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Olearia ramulosa is a member of the family Asteraceae. It is a flowering plant native to Australia, and is classified as a shrub. The plant's classification is further divided into the genus Olearia, which includes over 200 species of shrubs. Olearia ramulosa is a unique species within this genus due to its distinctive growth habit and leaf shape. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary history and its relationship to other flowering plants in the Asteraceae family.
